title: refund — kill switches
description: Operational kill switches distinct from the tier-based `features.json` gates. These are for emergency-off / staged rollout / per-tenant ops interventions. Every flag is ON in normal production; off flips are ops decisions.

flags.description: Master toggle for InitiateProviderRefundJob. Off = every provider dispatch call refuses with 503 REFUND_DISPATCH_MAINTENANCE. Refunds remain in status='approved' until the flag flips back on. Emergency-only (used during payment-module maintenance windows). Approve/reject/cancel paths still work; only provider dispatch pauses.

Off = the requires_approval computation returns false for every refund. Every refund auto-approves. Emergency-only when the approval workflow is misbehaving (e.g. approver notifications broken). Small tenants with `refund_approval_workflow` entitlement off already have this behaviour without the kill switch.

Off = RefundSucceeded does NOT fire RefundClawbackReferral / RefundClawbackCoupon downstream. Referral rewards + coupon redemptions do NOT reverse. Emergency-only if the cascade is misbehaving — admin manual reversal via referrals + coupon module CLIs then required.
Off = RefundSucceeded does NOT fire marketing::RefundIssued. Ad-network ROAS adjustment pauses (retains inflated ROAS values). Used when marketing module is unstable.
Off = ReconcileRefundsJob + CheckRefundProviderStatusJob + PurgeExpiredRefundDataJob skip. Only flip off for maintenance windows.
Off = POST /refunds/mine returns 503 REFUND_CUSTOMER_SELF_SERVICE_MAINTENANCE. Admin path still works. Used during customer-facing UI outages when we want to redirect requests via support.

Off = FinalizeRefundJob skips the credit note + transaction + clawback + marketing steps. Refund still transitions to succeeded but WITHOUT downstream side effects. EMERGENCY-ONLY — leaves accounting in an inconsistent state until manual reconciliation. Never flip off without a follow-up refund:generate-credit-note run.
Off = the refund-volume report endpoint returns 503. Used when the report generation is under load or the underlying query is unhealthy.
